article_title: Google Closes $32 Billion Wiz Acquisition, Its Largest Deal Ever, as Cloud Security Consolidation Accelerates

content_preview.title: Google Closes $32 Billion Wiz Acquisition, Its Largest Deal Ever, as Cloud Security Consolidation Accelerates
content_preview.summary: Google completed its all-cash acquisition of cloud security firm Wiz on March 11, clearing regulatory reviews in seven jurisdictions and reshaping the competitive landscape for independent cybersecurity vendors.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Google on March 11 completed its $32 billion all-cash acquisition of Wiz, the cloud security platform founded in Israel in 2020, closing the largest deal in Alphabet's history after a year-long regulatory gauntlet that spanned seven jurisdictions. The transaction, which [previously faced intense scrutiny](/article/2026-02/08-eu-faces-defining-moment-as-february-10-deadline-looms-for-googles-32-billion-wiz-acquisition) from European antitrust regulators, ultimately received unconditi...

editor_notes.source_verification: TechCrunch confirms: $32B all-cash close on March 11, regulatory clearances across 7 jurisdictions, $1B ARR in 2025, ~1800 employees, multi-cloud commitment, independent brand retention. EU Commission decision and Google blog post provide primary source corroboration.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified: deal value, close date, regulatory jurisdictions, Wiz ARR, employee count, Rappaport leadership. Competitive context (Palo Alto/CyberArk, CrowdStrike/SGNL) adds useful market landscape.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Balanced reporting covering both the deal's significance and critics' structural conflict concerns. No promotional language.
editor_notes.originality: Strong coverage with six sources and good internal cross-reference to prior EU regulatory coverage. The conflict-of-interest analysis is editorially valuable.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Comprehensive M&A news at 782 words. Six sources including primary (EU Commission, Google blog). Well-balanced between deal facts and market implications.
